summary: Inspect or annotate ignored local AW session logs.

inputs.name: session_log_command
inputs.description: Generated parser destination for the selected session-log subcommand.

inputs.name: text
inputs.description: Runtime field populated only by the note subcommand.

inputs.name: path
inputs.description: Optional session log path populated by the analyze subcommand.

inputs.name: id
inputs.description: Optional session id or aw-session filename populated by the analyze subcommand.

inputs.name: segment
inputs.description: Optional segment id populated by the analyze subcommand.

inputs.name: no_artifacts
inputs.description: Optional export flag that excludes raw-output artifacts.

writes.when: create or rotate the current local session log
create a local session log or append an optional note
repair or backfill a local session-log index
create an explicit share-safe export bundle

steps.description: Read status, create a new session, append a note, analyze or repair an index, or export a share-safe bundle.

guards: Do not capture non-AW shell output.
Do not treat local session logs as proof, Planning state, Memory, or closeout authority.
Session-log analysis is local dogfooding evidence until promoted intentionally.
Repair preserves existing indexed entries and artifacts; export never mutates originals.
Share-safe export redacts known local paths but does not claim arbitrary secret detection.
proof: generated command package checks include the session-log surface
session logging tests cover status, note, index, artifact, redaction, and analysis behavior through the generated CLI
